reason: Response A delivers an actual joke with the setup/punchline Q&A structure Sonnet reliably uses when asked for "a joke," plus a fresh, internally coherent conceit (Monday blamed for weekend crimes) that fits the "original" request. Response B reads as a polished poetic aphorism rather than a joke, lacking a punchline; that metaphor-heavy, literary framing is a common tell of a model imitating a "sophisticated" style rather than producing the comedic payoff Sonnet prioritizes.
